Tewksbury Public Library services are available to anyone who enters the building; a valid library card or identification is required to take materials out of the building. For the fastest service, an individual should present their own library card each time materials are checked out. If the individual forgets to bring their library card, the individual can present government-issued photo identification in lieu of the library card. Each library card holder must comply with any borrowing restrictions linked to the borrower's card. Residents of other Merrimack Valley Library Consortium ( MVLC ) libraries must present a current, valid library card or identification from their hometown library. Please see our Library Card Policy for complete details about acquiring and using a TPL Library Card. The Tewksbury Library is a member of the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC) , which is made up of 35 libraries in Massachusetts' Merrimack Valley. How to get a Tewksbury library card: Stop by the Circulation Desk during regular operating hours with identification showing your name and current address and complete a short library card application form. Children under 14 years of age must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian when applying for a library card. Print and fill out a Library Card Application Form (para español, haga clic aquí) and bring it to the Circulation Desk with identification showing your name and current address. Children under 14 years of age must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian when applying for a library card. How to get a MVLC eCard: Complete the online eCard application here An eCard is a Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC) library card that allows you immediate access to library collections, including books, DVDs, video games, and more, electronic resources, including e-books, e-audiobooks, e-magazines, streaming music and video, and research databases. An eCard is for patrons who do not already have an MVLC library card. You are eligible to apply for an MVLC library eCard if you are a resident of Massachusetts, and do not currently have a library card from an MVLC member library. If you are unsure of your library card number and/or pin, please contact the Circulation Desk at 978-640-4490, ext. 202 to speak with a staff member about gaining access. Renew your library card: Your TPL library card expires every three years as a means of confirming your address and contact information are up to date. To renew your card, you can do so online here by providing your barcode and PIN/password. Need help renewing your card? Call the Circulation Desk at 978-640-4490, ext. 202 for assistance. pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.

Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Each pass admits up to 4 people at 1/2 price admission.
IMPORTANT – NEW INFORMATION! After receiving an email with the library discount pass from the library, you must then reserve your visit online with Boston Children’s Museum.
• Visit https://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing
• Review the directions for timed tickets
• Click on the "Discount Programs" & drop down arrow
• Click on "Half Price Library Timed Ticket " to book
• NEW - PlaySpace Exhibit Reservations. Online reservations are required to visit PlaySpace.
• The date on the Library Discount Pass must match the date of the your visit.
• Once arriving at the Museum, proceed to the Admissions Scanning Station with your online reservation and the date specific Library Discount Pass received from the library.
• Contact Orders@BostonChildrensMuseum.org with questions.
You save by using this pass! Admission without this pass is:
Children (0-12 months): FREE
Purchased by the TPL using funding from the State Aid to Public Library program administered by the MA Board of Library Commissioners. 
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass

There is a public picnic area and free parking is available."} The Butterfly Place 120 Tyngsboro Road, Westford, MA 01886978-392-0955https://www.butterflyplace-ma.com/[Details/Reserve]
The Butterfly Place A winding pathway through an atrium filled with flowers, koi fish, and quail allows visitors to walk among butterflies from all over the world. Staff members are available for questions and “show and tell” demonstrations. A continuously running video explains the butterfly life cycle, and exhibits enable visitors to observe eggs, caterpillars, cocoons and chrysalides with information about each species. A gift shop is on the premises. There is a public picnic area and free parking is available.
Categories: Children, Family, Nature, Science
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits Pass allows one free admission.
This is a coupon pass and can be picked up at the Library up to 1 week in advance. You do not need to return this pass.
Seniors (65+): $13.00
Children (3-12 years): $11.00
Children (2 and under): FREE
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-discovery-museums_4_md.jpg","telephone": "978-264-4200","name": "Discovery Museum","url": "","description": "Acton&apos;s Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum&apos;s building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles. "} Discovery Museum 177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720978-264-4200http://www.discoveryacton.org/[Details/Reserve]
